Job Title: Senior Portfolio Manager, Fixed Income & Debt
Ad Text:
Company Description


Supporting millions of Aussies since 1988 with low fees and competitive long-term performance. Profits back to members, not shareholders

Closing date: 1st May 2026

Please note Rest does not accept speculative resumes from recruitment agencies 

Rest will review applications prior to the closing date and may close the role earlier 

Job Description


Established in 1988, Rest is one of Australia’s largest profit-to-member superannuation funds.

We support more than two million members, with around $100 billion of funds under management and are recognised as a responsible investment leader*. We believe when members understand and engage with their super, they’re more likely to get a better retirement outcome.

Everything we do at Rest is underpinned by our values and behaviours, we want to Be Daring, Keep it Simple, Take Action and Have Grit. To put it simply we want our people to thrive and love the work they do.

You will lead the portfolio management of the internal Australian Fixed Income portfolio to ensure it delivers its risk and return objectives in the context of Rest’s broader investment beliefs and whole of fund requirements. You will enhance the investment process with a focus on quantitative and qualitative research, data quality, ESG integration, portfolio construction, risk management and efficiency.

Key accountabilities:

Lead a team of one Senior Analyst and support the development of other Fixed Income and Debt team members.
Contribute to Whole of Fund investment thinking by working collaboratively with the broader Fixed Income & Debt team, Listed Assets, Asset Allocation team, Responsible Investments team and Governance team.
Back up and support the management of the internal Cash portfolio.
Represent the Australian internal Fixed Team when meeting with internal and external stakeholders.
Take primary responsibility for the investment strategy, portfolio construction and day‑to‑day management of the internal Australian fixed income portfolio to deliver its risk and return objectives.
Lead fundamental and quantitative research on interest rate and credit markets, including macroeconomic, valuation and technical drivers.
Ensure full adherence to Rest’s regulatory requirements, internal policies and mandate guidelines.  

Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in finance or related discipline
Ideally post-graduate qualifications e.g. Masters, CFA

Required experience, understanding or credentials including:

Demonstrated, relevant experience with an excellent track record in managing Australian fixed income portfolios in either investment management and/or superannuation
Extensive knowledge and understanding of global fixed income markets, macroeconomic analysis, portfolio theory and designing investment solutions.
Extensive experience in portfolio management and trading fixed income and cash investments across physical and derivative markets.

Additional Information


Our benefits have been designed so you can tailor your experience with us and include:

Personal and professional development opportunities 
Hybrid working 
Purchase leave scheme and gender neutral 16 weeks paid parental leave 
Super Contribution Continuation for 12 Months of parental leave 
Linkedin Learning 
Income Protection Insurance 
Rest Excellence awards (peer recognition awards based on Rest’s values and behaviours) 
Rest Stops - meeting free breaks 

If you share our values, believe you can help make a difference for our members and want to be part of a leading superannuation fund with a Super culture, please click Apply Now.

Rest is committed to creating a flexible work environment and culture that embraces diversity, equity, and inclusion - where people feel welcome, safe to be themselves and inspired to do their best.

We value the different backgrounds, lived experiences and abilities our diverse team brings. We welcome and encourage applications from candidates of all ages, cultural backgrounds, faiths, gender identities, sexual orientations and thinking styles. This includes people with disability, neurodiverse individuals, Aboriginal & Torres Strait Islander peoples and those with disrupted work history due to career or other breaks.

Please note only people with the right to work in Australia will be considered.

*Funds under management as at 31 July 2025. Rest is recognised as a Responsible Investment Leader by the Responsible Investment Association Australia (RIAA) in its Responsible Investment Benchmark Report 2022.

Job Title: Manager, Solutions Development, Treasury & Investments Implementation

Ad Text:

Design, build, and maintain data pipelines to ingest, transform, and curate data from multiple enterprise and project data sources.

The chance to play a key role in an Enterprise Level Project

Flexibility with working from home and in the office

How to apply:

Company: Good to Great Schools Australia Limited
Ad Text:
Location: Australia, 100% Remote or Cairns Based

About Good to Great Schools Australia (GGSA) 

Good to Great Schools Australia is a dynamic and supportive organisation committed to improving educational outcomes across Australia. We partner with schools and school systems to guide them on their improvement journey from Poor to Fair, Fair to Good, and Good to Great. Our experienced team provides extensive training, coaching, data monitoring, and tools to ensure each partner school develops capable teachers and delivers effective instruction to every child. 

About the Role 

As a Business Analyst at GGSA, you will play a critical role in bridging the gap between business needs and technology solutions. You will be responsible for coordinating with senior stakeholders, designers, and development teams to gather and write detailed requirements, ensuring all project outcomes align with our strategic goals. Additionally, you will take on testing responsibilities and oversee organisational reporting to support data-driven decision-making. 

Key Responsibilities 

Requirement Gathering & Documentation: Collaborate with senior stakeholders to elicit, document, and refine business requirements. Translate these requirements into comprehensive user stories and functional specifications. 

Stakeholder Coordination: Act as a liaison between business units, designers, and technical teams to ensure clear communication and alignment throughout the project lifecycle. 

Testing & Quality Assurance: Develop and execute test plans, manage testing activities, and ensure all deliverables meet the required quality standards. Assist with testing, bug triage, and user acceptance testing (UAT) to ensure quality standards are met by coordinating with development teams and stakeholders to address issues efficiently. 

Organisational Reporting: Produce and manage regular reports on project progress, KPIs, and other relevant metrics, providing insights to senior management and stakeholders. 

Dashboarding & Visualisation: Develop and maintain dashboards using tools like Google Looker and Power BI to provide actionable insights and track key performance indicators. 

Risk Management: Provide support by identifying Identify project risks, develop mitigation strategies, and provide regular status updates to governance bodies. 

Additional Responsibilities: Undertake other duties as required, including ad-hoc projects and tasks that align with business needs and contribute to overall organisational success. 

About You 

Innovative Problem Solver: Capable of creating solutions that meet diverse stakeholder needs while managing ambiguity and complexity. 

Customer-Centric: Strong focus on understanding and prioritising the customer journey, ensuring all solutions are aligned with customer needs. 

Key Skills and Experience 

Educational Background: Tertiary qualifications in Business, Engineering, or a related field. 

Experience: Minimum of 3 years in a similar role, such as Business Analyst, Product Owner, or Project Manager. 

Methodology Expertise: Strong understanding and practical experience with Agile and Waterfall 

Tools Proficiency: Skilled in using JIRA, Confluence, MIRO, Microsoft Teams, and other collaborative tools. 

Process Improvement: Demonstrated ability to manage processes effectively, focusing on documentation and continuous improvement. 

Communication Skills: Excellent interpersonal skills with the ability to engage stakeholders at all levels. 

Essential Criteria 

User Story Development: Ability to produce well-structured user journeys and write user stories in Gherkin format. 

Education Sector Experience: Familiarity with educational systems such as LMS and experience in the education sector. 

Advanced Problem-Solving: Strong analytical skills and resilience in high-pressure situations. 


Why Join Us? 

At GGSA, you’ll join a team that values transformation and excellence in education. We offer a collaborative environment with opportunities for professional growth, impacting our partners and shaping the future of education in Australia.

The Client

Our client are prominent ASX listed business with a a national presence and are considered industry leaders in their relevant field. They specialise in the production and distribution of products which service critical industries on a day to day basis. This role is sat within Group Finance and have visibility across the ELT as well as Divisional GMs with a specific focus on delivery of the Group's FP&A obligations as well as business partnering with key stakeholders.

The Role

Reporting into the Head of FP&A, you will be responsible for managing a small team and have responsibilities including but not limited to:

Preparation and delivery of monthly board reporting packages including operational performance and analysis of key business drivers
Preparation of the group's annual budget
Periodic forecasting/reforecasting
Trend and scenario analysis
Providing insights and decision support into the group’s long term strategic plan
Business partnering with finance and non-finance stakeholders
About You  

CA qualified with a minimum of 6 years total experience
Experience working in a large corporate environment (preferably within an ASX listed Group FP&A function) OR making your first move from a Big 4 Chartered Accounting firm (Corporate Finance, TS or M&A experience will be held in high regard)
Strong Excel skills
Competent with building and maintaining 3-way corporate models
Be able to build effective working relationships across multiple functions
Interested?

Email your CV to Mitch at mroberts@fourquarters.com.au or apply via the link provided.

Company: Talent – Specialists in tech, transformation & beyond
Ad Text:
About the role:

This role will work alongside the Information Governance team to roll out mandatory data classification and labelling procedures, in line with the state government's mandated WA Information Classification Policy. Further to this, the project will also deliver aspects of the corporation's Record Keeping Plan to improve records management and compliance.
The analyst will provide specialist expertise to deliver an enterprise wide roll out of information security classification. This could include PRIS/WAICP aligned classification frameworks, understanding of the use of metadata in EDRMS, the operation impacts and set up of sensitivity labels and retention controls in EDRMS.
The role will be required to facilitate and manage stakeholder engagement and workshops, document requirements and business cases for funding justifications and understands change impacts in such projects as well as having a good understanding/experience in compliance, risks reduction and business adoptions.

What the role will involve:

Workshop with stakeholders how mandatory labelling could be implemented, and co-designing an implementation approach
Updating policies, standards and procedures
Defining a backlog of activities that are delivered using an Agile scrum methodology
Tracking and reporting technical works commissioned to platform teams in IT
Testing technical changes and facilitating user acceptance testing

Key skills and experience:

Strong stakeholder engagement and management skills
Prior experience in delivering information classification projects in regulated environment
Good working knowledge of records/information lifecycle and sensitivity classifications including retention and disposal, metadata/controlled vocabularies
Experiencing in conducting information discovery across repositories, inventories including legacy content shares etc
Ability to analyse and map information stores to classification and record requirements in alignment to organisational policies, operational practices and legislative drivers
Strong understanding and capability in developing solutions for classification schemas and metadata requirements
Ability to document and write operational guidelines and supporting documentation e.g. work instructions
Demonstrated ability to work effectively as part of a collaborative team, whilst also operating independently and autonomously to meet project timelines and deliverables
Experience in developing training materials and conduct briefings or workshops to encourage adoption
Understands change management principles and can effectively support and manage small scale change initiatives

Desirable:

Experience in delivering classification projects in complex or regulated environments
Sound understanding of privacy and confidentiality legislation and organisation obligations
Background in information governance transformation and business improvements
Ability to navigate ambiguity and make informed and reasonable recommendations even if legacy systems or environment is unclear
Experience with stakeholder engagement
Strong communication and advisory skills

Overview
An opportunity exists for an experienced Data Governance Lead to establish and drive enterprise-wide data governance practices. This role will lead the design and implementation of frameworks that treat data as a strategic asset, ensuring high standards of data quality, compliance, metadata management, and master data integrity. Working cross-functionally, you will embed governance principles into business operations and support data-driven decision-making across the organisation.

Main Responsibilities
Develop and implement enterprise data governance frameworks
Define roles, responsibilities, and data ownership (RACI) models
Establish and monitor data quality standards, metrics, and processes
Lead data quality issue resolution and continuous improvement initiatives
Ensure compliance with data privacy regulations (e.g. GDPR, local laws)
Implement metadata management and data catalogue frameworks
Drive master data management (MDM) practices for key data domains
Technical Requirements
5+ years’ experience in data governance, MDM, or related roles
Strong knowledge of governance frameworks and best practices
Experience with data quality, metadata, and MDM tools (e.g. Collibra, Informatica, Alation, Reltio)
Understanding of regulatory and compliance requirements (e.g. GDPR, CCPA)
Experience developing governance artefacts (policies, standards, glossaries)
Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills
Relevant degree and/or certifications (e.g. CDMP, DCAM, CIPP)
If this sounds like the right opportunity for you, APPLY NOW.

Better and fairer care. Always.

St Vincent's has been a leader in Australia's health and aged care landscape for more than 165 years. Since our founders, the Sisters of Charity, opened our first hospital in 1857, our services and nearly 30,000 people have been behind some of Australia's most important medical breakthroughs. We are a microcosm of Australia's health and aged care system and are uniquely positioned to lead and respond to our rapidly changing environment.

Are you an experienced Data Engineering leader ready to shape the future of healthcare technology? Join St Vincent's Health Australia (SVHA) and lead our transformation journey as we build an integrated Data, AI & Interoperability Platform to modernise healthcare delivery. This is your opportunity to pioneer the next generation of healthcare data infrastructure.

The Opportunity

St Vincent's Health Australia (SVHA) is launching its future as a "Connected, Intelligent Healthcare organisation" to unify data, AI and intelligence across our national footprint. As the Data Engineering & Innovation Lead, you will be the platform owner for our modern data and AI platform, guiding St Vincent's through an exciting transformation to cloud & AI-native data capabilities.

This is a unique opportunity to architect and lead the engineering function as we embark on our journey toward real-time IoT data integration, Generative AI and Machine Learning, self-service analytics, and data democratisation across our healthcare ecosystem.

You and your team will be responsible for bring structured and unstructured data from multiple sources at many speeds, transforming it into meaningful inputs for advanced analytics and AI. You will also be leading the establishing and growth of cutting edge AI capabilities as St Vincent's navigates its journey to increasingly embedding AI into the services it provides to its patients and residents.

Working closely with diverse stakeholders across clinical, operational and corporate functions, you'll build strong partnerships and implement process improvements that unlock the power of data to enhance patient care and operational excellence.

This role is part of a broader Data Analytics Community across SVHA, and all activities must align with SVHA's Data Governance & Analytics Strategy and Standards, ensuring we build a sustainable, scalable platform for the future.

What you will be doing.

Key Responsibilities

As Data Engineering & Innovation Lead, you will be expected to:

Serve as the technical platform owner for our modern Data & AI platform. Lead the design, implementation and evolution of data engineering solutions that enable real-time data processing, IoT integration, and advanced analytics capabilities across the organisation.
Drive innovation through Generative AI and Machine Learning capabilities, establishing frameworks and pipelines that enable predictive analytics, intelligent automation and data-driven decision making to transform patient care and operational efficiency.
Enable data democratisation by building robust, user-friendly data pipelines and self-service analytics capabilities that empower stakeholders across the organisation to access and leverage data insights independently and securely.
Design and implement cloud-native data architectures including data lakes, lakehouses, streaming platforms and interoperability layers. Establish data modelling standards, ETL/ELT processes and data quality frameworks that support scalable, reliable data operations.
Build strong relationships with clinical, operational and technical stakeholders to understand data needs, gather requirements and implement process change management. Act as a trusted advisor on data engineering best practices and innovation opportunities.
Ensure the Mission, Vision, Creed, and Values of SVHA (Compassion, Justice, Integrity, Excellence) are effectively integrated into all areas of responsibility, maintaining ethical data practices and patient-centered technology solutions.

What You'll Bring.

Essential Experience & Skills:

Tertiary qualification Degree in Data Science, Statistics, Computer Science, or a related field
Extensive experience in data engineering, data architecture, and modern cloud-native data platforms (Azure preferred, Fabric, databricks, snowflake desirable)
Proven track record of leading data engineering initiatives and serving as a platform owner for enterprise data solutions
Strong expertise in data modelling, ETL/ELT pipelines, data warehousing, and real-time data processing
Experience with AI/ML platforms, including Generative AI integration and MLOps practices
Demonstrated ability to design and implement self-service analytics and data democratisation initiatives
Deep understanding of data governance, data quality frameworks, and interoperability standards (particularly in healthcare)
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills, with ability to translate technical concepts for diverse audiences
Experience driving innovation and continuous improvement in complex organisational environments

Desirable:

Data, infrastructure and/or AI certifications by recognised providers (Microsoft, , Databricks, Snowflake etc.)
Knowledge and experience of the health industry and healthcare data standards (HL7, FHIR)
Experience with IoT data platforms and real-time streaming technologies
Track record of leading digital transformation initiatives in large, complex organisations

If you are driven to achieve results, possess strong technical leadership and innovation skills, and want to contribute to a workplace that is safe and promotes personal wellbeing while transforming healthcare through data and technology, we encourage you to apply.

Things that work for you:

Opportunity to lead the engineering transformation for a major healthcare organisation
Ability to visibly see your impact on patient care and health service delivery
Work with cutting-edge technologies including AI, ML, IoT and modern cloud platforms
Competitive salary packaging options
Discounted private health insurance

There’s never been a better time to be in energy. And there’s never been a more exciting time to be at Endeavour Energy.

More than 2.7 million people across New South Wales rely on us every day for the supply of safe and reliable power to their homes and businesses. We employ more than 1,700 people across our catchment, making us one of the largest employers in Sydney’s Greater West.

We need talented people who care about one another and the work they do. Why? Because we’re one of Australia's fastest growing networks, connecting 20,000 new customers each year, including the Western Sydney Aerotropolis. And when we power together, we go further, faster; towards a smarter, more sustainable future for us all.

About the role

We have an exciting opportunity for a Senior Treasury Manager to play a pivotal role in the transformation of Endeavour Energy’s treasury function, and, in doing so, helping us build a smarter, more resilient grid for the future.

This dynamic, commercially focused position supports the Treasurer and Assistant Treasurer to deliver best-practice capital planning, funding, liquidity management and financial risk management. You’ll work closely with financial and operational peers to help shape long-term financial strategy which improves commercial outcomes, while ensuring we continue to operate at the highest standards of financial integrity and performance.

The Senior Treasury Manager will apply treasury expertise together with commercial acumen and a mindset of continuous improvement, to make material contributions to a diverse range of treasury activities, from complex loan and capital market transactions to developing and executing hedging programs, and integrating automation and AI into treasury workflows. This position is the lynchpin between strategy and hands-on practical implementation.

Key accountabilities
Provide recommendations and analytical support to the Treasurer and the Assistant Treasurer to improve the management of all treasury activities including capital planning, funding, liquidity, hedging and reporting.
Monitor financial markets and perform scenario analyses to improve debt portfolio management.
Draft papers for review as directed, including Board papers, which are timely, succinct, free of jargon, compelling and at the right level for the target audience.
Partner with and influence other leaders, collaborating on strategic and tactical initiatives by applying treasury expertise to improve commercial outcomes, drive results, add value and realise efficiencies.
Apply a high level of attention to detail in quantitative and qualitative analyses, including when assessing terms and conditions in bank loan documentation, and developing information packs for, and responses to questions from, investors, banks and credit rating agencies.
Establish and maintain effective relationships with key external stakeholders such as investors, credit rating agencies, financial institutions, advisors, lawyers and counterparties.
Take a leading, hands-on role in improving cash management and forecasting, working capital management, capital management, and financial risk management to best-practice standard.
Identify, lead and implement process improvement initiatives, including increasing system utilisation through process digitalisation, automation and AI adoption, while ensuring effective internal controls and compliance with Policy.
About you

You are a commercially astute treasury professional with strong market knowledge. You apply exceptional analytical capability to gain unique insights which inform data-driven strategy development. You can explain complex, technical concepts in a succinct and compelling way to non-technical audiences. You thrive in fast-paced environments, bring innovation to complex problems, and enjoy collaborating with stakeholders at all levels.

Minimum Qualifications
Bachelor’s degree in Commerce, Business, Economics or other quantitative discipline
Eligible for membership of the Australian Corporate Treasury Association.
Skills & Experience
Significant experience in treasury roles in large and/or complex organisations.
Sound understanding of, and deep experience in working on financial instruments including derivatives, loan and capital markets transactions, interest rate and FX risk management, and cash management and forecasting.
Commercially savvy with a continuous improvement mindset.
Using advanced financial modelling to gain unique analytical insights which inform strategy development and support data-driven decision making.

Optus is an Australian telecommunications company, providing over 11 million services daily to our customers through mobile, broadband, and digital solutions. Our team focuses on delivering outstanding service and innovative offerings to our customers. We are looking for a motivated and highly skilled Senior Demand Forecasting Analyst to join us and support our contact centres.

What You'll Do | Your day to day will consist of

Developing and maintaining advanced processes to research and collate insights that support the Demand Forecasting Process across various channels.

Working closely with stakeholders such as the Transformation team, insights communities, channel leads, initiative owners, and partners. We ensure forecasted demand and workforce models are understood, agreed upon, and supported.

Providing detailed trend analysis of call volumes, shrinkage, occupancy, and other metrics imperative to understanding demand drivers or variance.

Managing various data points and reports to build meaningful intelligence related to demand, capacity, leave management, and work allocation.

Conducting in-depth analysis of initiatives and making recommendations for future inclusion in demand assumptions.

Identifying workforce improvement opportunities and driving guideline implementation to deliver an optimised workforce without negatively impacting the customer.

Owning and governing long-term demand assumptions, scenarios and sensitivities to support workforce strategy, budgeting and investment decisions.

Developing and presenting scenario-based insights and "what-if" modelling to support executive decision-making and business trade-offs.

Translating complex forecasting outputs into clear, actionable narratives for senior leaders, confidently challenging assumptions where required.

Driving continuous improvement in forecast accuracy, bias reduction and demand modelling methodologies across channels.

What You Bring | What makes you perfect for the role?

Extensive experience with demand forecasting and planning in large complex environments.

Strong analytical expertise to determine opportunity areas and drive optimised rostering.

Demonstrated ability to influence senior stakeholders and challenge business assumptions using data-driven insights.

Strong experience in scenario modelling, sensitivity analysis and long-range planning to support strategic workforce and financial decisions.

Proven capability in operating within complex, multi-channel contact centre environments with high variability and competing priorities.

Proven interpersonal skills with high-quality written and verbal communication.

Proven achievement in a comparable position, with the capability to develop and guide effective teams.

Experience in crafting and managing complex forecast models, including advanced statistical analysis.

Proficiency in using Tableau, Excel, PowerBI and other reporting and workforce analytics tools.

Solid understanding of Workforce Planning (WFP) principles and metrics.

The good stuff…. | What's in it for you?

Competitive remuneration and colleague discounts. Make life easier (and more affordable) with $80 monthly credit and 25% off Optus products and outstanding shopping discounts with our retail partners.

Flexible working arrangements with opportunities to work three days in the office, two days remote or home.

Vibrant and collaborative office campus that includes cafes, convenience store, chill out zones, GP, post office, gym, and on-site childcare center.

Competitive leave policies, including additional 'Connected' days to focus on culture, family, health, community, or whatever's important to you.

We support growing families with inclusive, carer-neutral paid Parental Leave of up to 16 weeks.

Direct your own development and build future-ready skills with Optus U's industry-focused micro-credentials, developed with La Trobe and Macquarie Universities.

Build meaningful connections through colleague-led networks and diversity initiatives including Culture Connect, Elevate Women, Disability Network, and Express Yourself (LGBTQIA+).

Put your wellbeing first with free access to counseling and support services, 24/7 — in-person, by phone, SMS, or video.

Free Optus bus from Macquarie University Metro Station (every 6-8 minutes); morning and afternoon.

Ad Text:
Exciting new leadership role
Unlock career growth opportunities across a market leading technology business.
Join a new, dynamic business division at Domain creating market leading content, data and insights


About Domain and CoStar Group


Domain is one of Australia's most trusted and loved property brands, helping millions of Australians find their dream home and stay informed about the property market.

Now, Domain has joined forces with CoStar Group (NASDAQ: CSGP), a global leader in real estate information, analytics, and online marketplaces to take the next big leap in property technology and data innovation.

As part of CoStar Group, an S&P 500 and NASDAQ 100 company with more than 35 years of experience transforming how people understand and transact real estate, Domain is entering an exciting new era of growth. CoStar is investing heavily in new products, technology, and people to revolutionise how Australians access property data, insights, and marketplaces.

This is a rare opportunity to join a world-class research team at the intersection of two powerful brands - Domain's local market expertise and CoStar's global scale and technology - as we build the future of real estate intelligence together.


Role Overview


The Market Research Manager leads a team of researchers in maintaining accurate and comprehensive commercial real estate market data and portfolio analytics. This role is central to CoStar Group's mission, enabling commercial real estate clients to make informed, high-impact business decisions that shape a vital segment of the economy helping businesses successfully open offices, retail stores, and industrial facilities. You will be responsible for leading the team in investigating and profiling companies to ensure owners, brokers, and real estate professionals can identify the best-suited buildings for their needs. Join our growing research organisation where your work makes a daily difference and is supported by industry-leading benefits and salaries.

This is an in-office position, Monday through Friday.


Key Responsibilities

Lead and Develop a High-Performing Research Team: Oversee a team of 8+ Associate Research Consultants, providing leadership, coaching, and mentorship to foster a strong team culture and a centre of excellence in line with individual development plans.
Ensure Data Integrity and Market Coverage: Supervise the collection, analysis, and timely publication of accurate, comprehensive commercial real estate data for your assigned markets/region, developing workflows and conducting regular audits to maintain the highest quality standards and client service.
Drive Strategic Planning and Reporting: Partner with sales and product teams to align with client needs, prepare quarterly "Territory Plans" for data and relationship improvements, and oversee the creation of comprehensive market reports.

Required Qualifications

Education: Bachelor's degree in Business, Real Estate, or a related field.
Experience & Skills:


2+ years of experience leading a team of 6 or more individuals.
2+ years in a professional, corporate environment.
2+ years of experience within commercial real estate.
Other: Flexibility to work shifts aligned with national time zones.


Preferred Qualifications and Skills

Demonstrated interest and/or experience in the commercial real estate industry.
Experience with creating effective Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) and meeting performance metrics (results-oriented).
Strong leadership and management skills, including coaching, mentoring, and workload control.
Success in building strong, cohesive teams through influence and collaboration.
Ability to articulate and execute corporate strategy effectively.
Proficiency in Excel, including the ability to manipulate data, filter, and develop basic formulas.
Creative problem solving, attention to detail, objectivity, and strategic thinking.
Analytical approach to interpreting information and trends.
Ability to interact at all levels and establish rapport with stakeholders.
Strong organisational skills.
5+ years of experience in real estate research or data management.


Why Join Us?

Be part of a global leader in property technology and data innovation.
Work with cutting-edge tools and methodologies to shape the future of real estate intelligence.
Enjoy career growth opportunities within a high-performing, collaborative team.


Equity, Diversity & Inclusion


Domain is enthusiastically and unapologetically committed to fostering an equitable, inclusive work culture which reflects our customers and communities. We are proactively looking for candidates from all lived experiences, including people with disability, and people of all ages, ethnicities, cultures (including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples), faiths, sexual orientations, and gender identities (including trans and non-binary people).


We are committed to providing an equitable recruitment process for people with disability. If you require adjustments during the process we're here to support. If you wish to receive this job advertisement in an accessible format, or have a confidential chat about workplace adjustments, please contact our Equity, Diversity and Inclusion team at edi@domain.com.au or leave a message on 1300 858 356 and we will get back to you.

Your new company

Join a large Federal Government department responsible for delivering nationally significant reforms that directly impact Australians and their access to essential services. The organisation operates at scale, works closely with central agencies, and places a strong emphasis on evidence-based decision-making and sound financial governance.



Your new role

You'll play a pivotal role in shaping funding and budget decisions for a nationally significant program. Working closely with senior leaders and key stakeholders, you will:

Take end-to-end ownership of a complex demand and cost model that underpins budget strategy, forecasting and policy decisions.
Develop detailed costings and projections to support New Policy Proposals, budget bids and funding submissions.
Maintain, update and refine an Excel-based financial model, ensuring assumptions, drivers and outputs remain robust and transparent.
Analyse expenditure trends and forecast future demand, monitoring actuals against projections to inform funding adjustments.
Translate complex financial and modelling information into clear, well-structured advice for executives and central agencies
Partner with internal stakeholders to align funding with program demand and operational delivery
Drive continuous improvement by enhancing models, processes, documentation and standard operating procedures


What you'll need to succeed

You're a confident finance professional who enjoys working with complexity and ambiguity. You'll bring:

Strong experience in financial modelling, costings, forecasting or demand analysis
A solid understanding of the Federal Budget cycle and related financial processes
Advanced Excel capability and experience with financial management systems
The ability to translate complex financial concepts into clear, well-written advice
Strong organisational skills and the ability to manage competing priorities
Experience in government, public sector consulting, or large complex organisations will be highly regarded.


What you'll get in return

You'll be part of a collaborative and purpose-driven team working on nationally significant reforms. This role offers EL1-level responsibility, exposure to senior decision-makers, opportunities to shape policy outcomes through financial insight, and the chance to further develop your leadership and modelling expertise within the APS.



What you need to do now

If you're interested in this position, please contact Alden Soe on (02) 6112 7698 or email alden.soe@hays.com.au to discuss the role in confidence.

Job Title: Senior Data Enablement Analyst-Medicare Mental Health Check In
Ad Text:
Real Impact at Scale -Your work will directly influence clinical and operational decision-making across a national health system - improving patient outcomes, service delivery, and performance at scale.
Own and Shape the Analytics Function-Move beyond reporting into true enablement - acting as a product owner for analytics assets, shaping KPI frameworks, and embedding data-driven decision-making across the organisation.
Work at the Intersection of Data, Strategy and Healthcare Transformation-Engage with senior stakeholders, influence strategy, and contribute to system-wide transformation within one of Australia's largest and most respected health organisations.


What if your next role helped transform how data drives care across Australia?
St Vincent's Health Australia is building a future where data is not just reported - but actively shapes decisions, improves patient outcomes, and transforms how care is delivered.

We are now seeking a Senior Enablement Analyst to play a critical role in enabling data-driven decision-making across one of Australia's largest and most complex health organisations.

This is a rare opportunity to move beyond traditional reporting and step into a role where your insights directly influence clinical excellence, operational performance, and system-wide transformation.


About the Program - Medicare Mental Health Check-In
St Vincent's Health Australia has partnered with the Australian Government to deliver the Medicare Mental Health Check-In - a national digital mental health service designed to help Australians access care earlier and more easily.

This program brings together multiple national partners across technology, clinical workforce, digital innovation and lived experience to deliver a new model of accessible, scalable mental health support.

As part of this broader transformation, data and analytics play a critical role in enabling service performance, improving outcomes, and ensuring the program delivers meaningful impact at scale.


The Opportunity
As a Senior Enablement Analyst, you will sit at the intersection of business and technology - acting as the critical bridge between data, systems, and decision-making.

You will partner with clinical, operational, and corporate teams to bring data to life - translating complex information into meaningful insights that shape how performance is understood, measured, and improved.

Your work will ensure leaders have the clarity and confidence to make informed decisions that enhance care, improve efficiency, and support innovative, digitally enabled services - including national mental health initiatives.

This isn't just a reporting role - it's about storytelling, influence, and embedding a culture where data drives better outcomes.

This is a 12 month fixed term-full-time role based in Melbourne (hybrid), with the opportunity to influence decisions at both operational and executive levels.

What You'll Do


Translate complex data into compelling insights and stories that influence decision-making
Design and deliver intuitive dashboards and visualisations using tools like Power BI
Develop and maintain high-quality, governed datasets across multiple systems
Partner with stakeholders to define meaningful KPIs and performance measures
Act as a product owner for analytics assets, managing priorities and continuous improvements
Champion data quality, governance, and best-practice analytics across the organisation
Support and uplift data literacy, helping teams confidently use data in their day-to-day work

About You
You are a highly capable analytics professional who thrives in complexity and enjoys turning data into meaningful, actionable insight.

You're confident engaging with stakeholders at all levels - from executives to technical teams - and are comfortable challenging assumptions to ensure data quality, measurement integrity, and the right outcomes are achieved.


You Bring:


8+ years' experience in analytics, business intelligence, or data enablement roles
Advanced skills in Power BI (or similar) and SQL
Strong understanding of data modelling, ETL processes, and data integration
Proven ability to translate business needs into actionable insights and visualisations
Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills
Experience working in complex, multi-system and multi stakeholder environments


Desirable


Experience in healthcare or highly regulated environments
Exposure to cloud-based data platforms (Azure, AWS)
Knowledge of data governance, privacy, and performance frameworks
Certifications in BI or analytics tools


Why St Vincent's?
This is more than a data role - it's an opportunity to influence how care is delivered across Australia.

At St Vincent's, you will:


Work on meaningful problems that impact patient outcomes and system performance
Be part of a purpose-driven organisation grounded in Compassion, Integrity, Justice and Excellence
Access salary packaging benefits to increase your take-home pay
Work in a collaborative, high-performing and mission-led environment
Have the opportunity to shape and mature analytics capability at scale

Ready to Make an Impact?
If you're passionate about data, driven by purpose, and excited to shape how insights improve healthcare, we'd love to hear from youPlease apply with your CV and cover letter, and respond to the following mandatory application questions:

Mandatory Application Questions
(Maximum 500 words per question)

1.Translating Data into Impact
Describe a project where you translated complex data into insights or visualisations that influenced decision-making. What tools did you use and what was the impact?

2. Stakeholder Engagement and Influence
Tell us about a time you worked with stakeholders who had differing priorities or perspectives. How did you manage this and what was the outcome?

3.Data Governance and Quality
Provide an example where you identified issues with data quality, definitions, or metrics. How did you address this and ensure integrity moving forward?


Please note: Applications are reviewed as they are received. The role may close early if a suitable candidate is identified.

Important Information
Your employment is subject to pre-employment checks, including relevant immunisations.

Equal Opportunity
We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities and abilities - including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, culturally and linguistically diverse communities, LGBTQIA+ communities and people with lived experience of mental health challenges. Inclusion is essential to our mission - and diversity reflects the community we serve.

Rabobank is the world’s leading specialist in food and agribusiness banking. One of our key strengths lies in our people who have a deep understanding of agriculture and are committed to adding long-term value for clients. Our commitment to our employees and clients is at the heart of everything we do.   

About the Role

Rabobank Australia & New Zealand is seeking a Data Ecologist to strengthen our sustainability capability and support our clients’ transition to a net‑zero and nature‑positive future.

This role is responsible for managing and evolving the data ecosystem used to capture, integrate, analyse and report customer climate and nature data. You will lead the development of innovative, bottom‑up data solutions that source information from agricultural production systems and the natural environment and integrate this data into existing and emerging bank systems.

Working closely with sustainability, data, technology and frontline teams, this role enables sustainability outcomes to be communicated clearly through reporting, portfolio analysis and client engagement. The Data Ecologist plays a critical role in supporting transition finance solutions and ensuring alignment with Australian and global sustainability strategies, reporting frameworks and Rabobank’s Road‑to‑Paris and Montreal commitments.

By providing high‑quality insights and technical expertise, this role helps frontline Managers incorporate sustainability propositions into client‑centred strategic discussions, strengthening Rabobank’s sustainability value proposition.

Key Accountabilities

In this role, you will:

Lead and manage strategic data solutions supporting sustainability reporting, portfolio analysis and decision‑making

Design and implement initiatives that accelerate the sourcing of high‑quality on‑farm emissions and nature data into banking systems

Develop and oversee data capture solutions for sustainability metrics, including climate and nature indicators

Work closely with the Data Systems & Governance Lead to ensure data quality, governance and alignment with business KPIs

Apply expertise in GIS, remote sensing, AI and emerging biodiversity data capture methods to sustainability challenges

Ensure sustainability reporting systems align with and complement existing bank data ecosystems

Track, manage and report sustainability goals and metrics within established frameworks

Provide technical input into sustainability strategy, policies, procedures and assessment processes

Support training and capability uplift related to sustainability KPIs and transition products

Contribute to the development of client transition finance solutions

Ensure data security, traceability and audit readiness

Drive continuous improvement in data quality, consistency, transparency and innovation

Share knowledge and best practice across the organisation

About You

You are a collaborative and commercially minded sustainability data specialist with a passion for enabling real‑world climate and nature outcomes. You are comfortable operating at the intersection of science, data and banking, and can translate complex technical concepts into practical business solutions.

Qualifications

Essential

University degree in Data Science, Geospatial Data Systems, Sustainability, Agribusiness, Finance, Business Administration, Science or a related field

Desirable

Postgraduate qualifications

Experience

Minimum 5 years’ experience in data systems, including GIS, remote sensing, AI or equivalent

Experience with sustainability, ecological or biodiversity assessment methodologies

Demonstrated experience implementing policies and executing strategic initiatives at scale

Proven experience working across cross‑functional, cross‑disciplinary projects

Knowledge & Skills

Strong understanding of climate science and ecosystem science

Knowledge of key sustainability themes, reporting frameworks and measurement systems

Advanced data analysis and systems integration skills

Subject matter expertise in integrating climate and nature data into financial reporting systems

Project management and stakeholder engagement capability

Excellent written and verbal communication skills

Ability to build strong relationships across local and international teams

Presentation and facilitation skills (training delivery desirable)

What we're proud to offer you: 

 Rabo@Anywhere is our hybrid way of working at Rabobank and for Australia, that means we have a blend of time working between the office and home.  

A competitive remuneration package, will be offered for the successful candidate. 

Wellbeing leave. These leave days support greater work/life balance and is just another way we are helping our employees to lead happier, healthier, and more fulfilling lives. 

Education Assistance Program. Rabobank values the development of its people and has a great Education Assistance Program to assist with professional development. 

Parental Leave that supports you and your family while giving you the freedom and flexibility to enjoy this special time (up-to 14 weeks paid leave) 

Bonus and Additional Leave. We realise sometimes 4 weeks Annual Leave isn't enough! At Rabobank we provide an incentive for eligible employees to receive 1 extra week Annual Leave and an option to purchase another 2 weeks Annual Leave. 

Extra Insurance. Rabobank recognises that employees need to protect their financial wellbeing, in the event of serious illness, injury, or even death. Rabobank provides eligible permanent employees with employer-funded Death, TPD and Income Protection Insurance. 

Rabo Workplace Giving program. As part of Rabobank's global Corporate Social Responsibility, Workplace Giving aims to encourage employees to contribute to our community. Rabo Workplace Giving program matches employee donations to selected Social Partners. 

 Our Values 

Rabobank Australia values inclusion, belonging, and positive experiences for all. Our work environment, our benefits, and the way we live our values, “Client Driven”, “Responsible”, “Professional” and “Cooperative” make it a great place to work. We welcome applicants from diverse backgrounds.

Job Title: Executive Lead - Strategy, Impact and Development
Ad Text:
Impactful community health organisation, addressing health and social inequality

Lead strategy translation, impact measurement and transformation

Strategic identification, assessment and pursuit of funding and growth opportunities




The Position

cohealth is seeking an accomplished and influential Executive Lead – Strategy, Impact and Development to steward the translation of an ambitious 10-year strategy into measurable impact for communities, funders and the broader health system. Described as an enterprise “conductor”, the Executive Lead will partner with the CEO and executive peers and take carriage of and continue to build disciplined performance monitoring, evaluation and benefits realisation practices, enabling the organisation to evidence value and impact. The Executive Lead will work with the business to commercialise and support growth of services, stewarding this work to ensure continued momentum. This position reports to the CEO and sits on the Executive team.

Success in this role will be demonstrated by:

Articulating the “why” of cohealth’s strategy, translating it into a compelling, human narrative that demonstrates impact on communities and the broader health system.

Augmenting consistent performance, monitoring and evaluation practices.

Stewarding service growth and commercialisation initiatives, ensuring alignment with values and mission.

Providing strategic leadership over communications, marketing and advocacy, ensuring evidence-based engagement with varied stakeholders.

Strengthening executive-level oversight of operating model redesign initiatives, particularly where sustainability and future models of care are being tested or piloted.

Equipped with senior level experience gained in private or NFP sectors, the Executive Lead will bring a strong commercial lens. Comfortable operating in ambiguity and with competing stakeholder expectations, the incumbent will have a proven ability in shaping and delivering organisational strategy and in the leadership of major initiatives. Possessing strong advocacy, diplomacy and influencing skills, they will offer the credibility to ensure the balance of workforce, community and government interests.

 

The Organisation

cohealth is a not-for-profit community health organisation delivering health and social support services across Melbourne’s CBD, northern and western suburbs, and the East Coast of Tasmania. The organisation exists to improve health and wellbeing and address health and social inequality, with a strong emphasis on reaching people experiencing barriers to health and wellbeing. Services are delivered through multidisciplinary, team-based models of care and span general practice (complex care), mental health, alcohol and other drugs, oral health, family violence, chronic disease management, refugee and asylum seeker health, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander health, homelessness and outreach programs, and selected aged care and community support services.

 

For more information on the organisation visit: https://www.cohealth.org.au/

Job Title: Health Information Management (HIM) Product Specialist (12 month Fixed Term)
Ad Text:
Our Mission
At iMedX, our mission is to provide solutions that give time and money back to care.

What we do
iMedX are industry leaders in clinical documentation and revenue cycle management solutions, and now our solutions leverage AI technology.  Driven by our commitment to innovation and quality, iMedX delivers trusted AI capabilities and expertise to the healthcare industry, providing healthcare providers with state-of-the-art tools to streamline their workflows.

iMedX Difference

At iMedX, our solutions are built on years of experience, deep expertise, and a thorough understanding of the healthcare landscape in Australia and New Zealand. Our services use Generative AI technology, acting as a companion to healthcare professionals to allow more time for patient care or for complex tasks that require human expertise.

Our values are built on Trust, Innovative Technology, Continuous Improvement, Integrity, Quality, a Customer-Focused, People-Centered approach, and delivering Value in everything we do.

About the Opportunity

We are on the lookout for a Health Information Management (HIM) Product Specialist to engage in an initial 12 month fixed term contract from June 2026 to June 2027.

This position will serve as a strategic advisor to the iMedX Products and Technology team, ensuring that cutting-edge, AI-driven Health Information Management solutions are developed, implemented, and embedded into real-world HIM and clinical coding operations. These solutions will include auto-coding, grouping, and AI-guided coding assignment functionalities within the HIM Companion Suite (HCS). The specialist will act as a Subject Matter Expert (SME) for Health Information Systems (HIS) and Revenue Cycle Management (RCM), collaborating closely with design, product, engineering, and implementation teams to deliver seamless, effective user experiences. The role will also support the commercialisation and successful customer adoption of iMedX’s HIM products in Australia and globally. 

As HCS progresses through active implementations, this role will help translate HIM operational requirements into scalable product capability, support implementations, add to the Product Roadmap and guide user-adoption. 

What You’ll Be Doing 

Strategic Advisory: 

Act as a HIM expert, providing continuous strategic input to the iMedX Products and Technology team. 

Contribute to the design and development of AI-driven HIM software solutions, particularly those addressing auto-coding, grouping, and AI-driven coding assignment needs. 

Driving HIM User requirements, facilitating these into easy to digest requirements for Product teams, and ensuring best coding practices are embedded throughout the User Experience.  

Product Development and Enhancement: 

Act as the HIM SME to assist the product team in designing, developing, and enhancing new software solutions focused on HIS and RCM. 

Engage with cross-functional teams to ensure the software design aligns with real-world HIM workflows and standards. 

Provide fast, practical, easy-to-understand answers to product and technology team questions, grounded in HIM practice. 

Attend product stand-ups and delivery ceremonies as required to unblock decisions and accelerate delivery. 

Implementation Enablement & Adoption (HCS): 

Partner with implementation teams to ensure product configuration and workflow design aligns to HIM operations (e.g., coding workflow orchestration, queue design, role-based access, and agreed ways of working). 

Support development of implementation collateral and role-based training content, ensuring it is fit-for-purpose for HIM users (coders, auditors, HIM leaders). 

Contribute to go-live readiness activities including workflow validation, adoption risk identification, and support model alignment. 

Provide HIM input into hypercare priorities and the continuous improvement backlog based on implementation feedback and real-world usage. 

User Acceptance Testing (UAT): 

Take a lead role in training users for User Acceptance Testing, acting on behalf of HIM users to ensure the software meets all functional and usability requirements. 

Define UAT scenarios aligned to HIM workflows, including edge cases and real-world operational constraints. 

Identify and troubleshoot issues raised during UAT and recommend practical solutions for improvement. 

Collaboration and User Experience Design: 

Collaborate with design teams to facilitate a new, intuitive user experience for HIM software products. 

Ensure that the user interface and overall software design are aligned with the workflow needs of HIM professionals. 

Role Interactions (Implementation-Stage Operating Model): 

Squad Leads: Partner with Squad Leads to clarify HIM requirements, validate workflow assumptions, support prioritisation decisions, and ensure HIM acceptance criteria is understood and testable throughout delivery. 

Senior HIM HCS Implementation Lead: Work closely with the Senior HIM HCS Implementation Lead to align product configuration with HIM operating models, support change management and adoption needs, and ensure implementation learnings flow back into product improvement. 

Senior AI PM (HCS Implementation Lead): Collaborate with the Senior AI PM to support end-to-end implementation outcomes by providing HIM SME input into solution readiness, adoption planning, deployment considerations, and feedback loops that ensure AI-enabled capability is trusted and embedded into HIM workflows. 

What You’ll Bring to iMedX

To thrive in this role, you’ll have:

Educational Background:

Bachelor’s degree in health information management, Healthcare Informatics, or related field. Advanced certifications in HIM, coding, or RCM would be advantageous. Agile Product Development or Project Management also desirable.  

Experience: 

Minimum of 10 years of experience working in HIM, preferably with experience in HIS and RCM solutions. 

Proven experience in Requirement Gathering and User Acceptance Testing (UAT) for HIM or clinical coding software. 

Experience with AI-driven coding solutions is highly desirable. 

Technical Knowledge:

Strong understanding of HIS and RCM processes, clinical coding, and auditing workflows.

Communication Skills:

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to convey technical and clinical concepts clearly to both technical teams and non-technical stakeholders. 

Customer Engagement:

Experience in product demonstration and providing strategic advisory to customers. 

Attention to Detail:

Ability to troubleshoot, analyze, and suggest improvements in software from a user’s perspective. 

Why Join iMedX?

At iMedX, we’re deeply committed to investing in our people. Our supportive, collaborative culture - extended to our growing virtual workforce - has led to an impressive eNPS score of 44 (January 2026). 

We’re proud of the positive, values-driven environment we’ve built at iMedX, where making decisions guided by our core values  is part of our everyday work.

As part of our team, you’ll benefit from:

Partner with some of the largest healthcare providers across ANZ, the US, and the Middle East 

Work on real AI in production, not pilots or proof of concept  

Combine deep healthcare expertise with advanced AI capability 

Be part of a growing organisation shaping the future of HIM and revenue cycle management

Employee Referral Program – Earn up to $2,000 for referring great talent

Access to iMedX’s AI Companion tool - cutting-edge Gen AI, this tool acts as an expert HIM in chat format to answer any question based on Australian Coding Standards and practices to help improve clinical documentation and revenue outcomes.

Flexibility – work remotely from home 

Work from Anywhere - up to 2 weeks per year you can "work from anywhere" within your usual domestic time zone.

Exclusive learning opportunities, Participate in bi-annual internal coder conferences and our iMedX annual Conference

Annual Conference and End of Year Party – We bring the entire team to Melbourne for an all-expenses-paid iMedX Conference, combining professional development with our year-end celebration. It’s our way of upskilling our team and showing that we genuinely value the time we spend together.

Opportunity to Have Your Say – We conduct bi-annual engagement surveys to track our eNPS and genuinely listen to our team’s feedback. We’re committed to taking action based on what you share

Career Growth – explore career pathways from Coding to Auditing and the opportunity to work across a diverse range of public and private health services in Australia and New Zealand

iMedX Study Support Program – Personalized support tailored to your individual career goals

Travel opportunities engage with clients onside when needed

Wellness Leave

Birthday leave

Employee Assistance Program – 24/7 access to a team of qualified psychologists when you need some additional support

Monthly (all in) Town Hall Meetings – to help you stay connected and informed

New Starter Welcome Pack – Kick off your iMedX journey with branded merch to make choosing your work attire effortless and fun

A supportive, inclusive culture that prioritizes collaboration 

Monthly ‘in house’ professional development sessions – coding questions answered in “real time” by experienced Coders, plus extensive library of education topics and more!

 Ready to Make a Difference with iMedX?” 
Join us in revolutionizing healthcare documentation and coding. Apply now to become part of a supportive, innovative team that values your expertise. Don’t miss this opportunity—apply today!”

The Role

Lead the establishment and uplift of TasWater’s data governance capability—setting clear standards, ownership and controls to improve data quality, trust, and regulatory compliance across the business.

This newly created role will drive the data governance framework, operating model, and roadmap, working with stakeholders across the business and technology to embed consistent definitions, stewardship, data quality practices, and lifecycle controls. You’ll enable better decision-making and delivery by ensuring critical data is well-managed, secure, and fit for purpose.

In this role, you will:

Develop and implement the enterprise data governance framework, standards, guidelines, and decision forums.

Establish data ownership and stewardship, partnering with data owners and SMEs to agree definitions, metadata and data products.

Define and monitor data quality measures and controls, driving remediation and continuous improvement for critical data.

Partner with Cyber, Architecture, and Delivery teams to embed data risk, classification, access and retention requirements into processes and platforms across Digital and Technology.

Lead and coach a small team to deliver governance, information management, and continuous improvement outcomes.

Improve document and content management practices (from accessibility to disposal), aligning information management with governance requirements and business needs.

You can influence senior stakeholders, balance risk and delivery outcomes, and translate privacy, security and regulatory requirements into practical controls. Ad Text:
About the Client

Our client is a well-established Australian organisation undergoing a significant transformation within its People & Culture function. Following the recent implementation of SuccessFactors, the organisation is focused on strengthening payroll governance, compliance, and data integrity across its national operations.

About the Role

We are seeking two Payroll Analytics Consultants to join a Payroll Governance team on an initial 6-month contract. This role will play a critical part in supporting ongoing payroll reviews, investigations, and remediation activities.

You will work across both legacy (TechOne) and current (SuccessFactors) systems, analysing complex payroll data sets and helping to identify and resolve discrepancies. This is a fast-paced environment requiring strong attention to detail and the ability to deliver insights under tight timeframes.

Key Responsibilities
Extract, cleanse, and reconcile payroll data across TechOne and SuccessFactors
Conduct detailed analysis to identify payroll variances (systemic and one-off)
Support payroll reviews, investigations, and remediation activities
Interpret awards, enterprise agreements, and payroll rules
Develop reports, dashboards, and documentation to support findings
Collaborate with internal stakeholders and external vendors
Contribute to time-sensitive investigative workstreams
What's in it for you?
Competitive day rate of $700-$800 (inclusive of super)
Opportunity to work on a high-profile payroll transformation program
National role with flexible working arrangements
Exposure to large-scale data analysis and remediation projects
Immediate start in a supportive, collaborative team
About You
Strong payroll operations knowledge, including pay runs and awards interpretation
Proven experience in payroll investigations, audits, or remediation
Advanced Excel skills (pivot tables, formulas, data reconciliation)
Experience with BI tools (Power BI/Tableau) highly regarded
Exposure to SuccessFactors and/or TechOne advantageous
Strong analytical mindset with high attention to detail
Ability to thrive in fast-paced, ambiguous environments

Candidates must have full Australian working rights.

Please call Nicky Hargreaves ASAP on +61 466173695 if of interest

Key aspects of the role

Flexible working conditions (almost 100 percent work from home)

5 days/week loading but less will be considered for the right applicant

All applicants must have Australian work rights

About us

A leader in delivering Microsoft data, Power Platform and Microsoft 365 services

Work with prestigious clients including government, multinationals and not-for-profits

Work with the latest technology in the Microsoft eco system

Fantastic culture with a genuine commitment to a healthy work-life balance

Work alongside a talented team of consultants who share your passion in building great solutions and learning new skills

Main responsibilities

Play a lead technical role in the delivery of Microsoft data solutions

Designing end-to-end Fabric, SQL, and Power BI solutions that integrate with a wide range of data sources/services including Microsoft 365, the Power Platform, Azure data services, Dynamics 365, and web APIs

Consulting with clients to gather reporting requirements, conduct wireframing, develop data visualisation content

Preparing and modelling data using Fabric, Power BI, SQL, and Analysis Services.

Resolving data preparation, modelling and visualisation challenges

Automating the distribution of Power BI content to users

Driving innovation across our data and analytics practice by uplifting our data engineering and visualisation capabilities

Key skills and experience required

7+ years of overall experience working on enterprise data challenges for medium to large organisations

5+ years of experience across Microsoft data and reporting tools

4+ years of experience working with Power BI

2+ years of experience working with pipelines in Data Factory, Synapse, or Fabric

1+ years of experience working with Microsoft Fabric

The ability to resolve data challenges using DAX and Power Query code.

Demonstrated experience using Azure data services including SQL Server, Storage, Key Vault and Synapse

Demonstrated experience of delivering Microsoft lakehouses and warehouses.

Knowledge of data visualisation concepts and good practices

Exposure to Power BI administrative concepts such as workspaces, gateways, permissions, and content delivery

Excellent listening skills and the ability to present and communicate effectively with project stakeholders

Experience working with others via Microsoft Teams

An understanding of data modelling concepts such as star schemas

It would be valuable if candidates have:

A Microsoft Fabric certification

A Microsoft Power BI certification

Consulting experience

Exposure to Tabular Editor, DAX Studio, or Analysis Services

Experience with Power Platform services including Automate and Power Apps

Experience in CI/CD or an Agile framework

Applying for the role

This is an urgent role to interview and start ASAP.

To apply for this role, please send your resume by clicking the APPLY button. All communication will be strictly confidential.

More than 50 years ago a foundation was laid, with a vision for the future. To re-imagine the environments where Australians would live, work and grow. In this time, Mirvac has evolved from a small joint venture to a thriving ASX-listed property group, leading in sustainability, innovation, safety, and placemaking.

Mirvac has created some of Australia's most iconic places, from thriving residential communities, to landmark offices and mixed-use precincts. At the heart of every project: a deep commitment to our customers, and an ambition to reimagine urban life.

At Mirvac, we know that it's our people, their passion and expertise and the exceptional experiences we create for our audience, that defines us.

Our opportunity

We have an opportunity for a Development Analyst to join our New Business team. You will primarily support the Commercial & Mixed-use New Business function (predominantly mixed-use development commercial and living orientated developments). (Internal job title: Analyst, Development New Business and Structured Products.).

The Commercial & Mixed-use New Business function is an integral part of Mirvac, tasked with replenishing Mirvac's large development pipeline and securing new opportunities and assisting with the transition of those projects in preparation for delivery. The Product Origination and Structured Projects team is also responsible for securing new capital sources to assist in the delivery of Mirvac's development pipeline and investment strategies.

Your responsibilities will include:

Assess new opportunities in residential, office, Build to Rent and retail that fit withing Mirvac's overarching strategy. Opportunities range from on-market large government led bids to off market partnerships or direct approached to existing landowners. Many opportunities are large mixed-use developments comprising at least two different asset classes.
Build and maintain financial models underpinning new business opportunities and investment strategies. This includes building development and investment models, running various scenarios as well as different transaction structures to test different strategies and producing succinct summaries of the results.
Ensure that each financial model is logical and in line with industry practice and in accordance with Mirvac agreed metrics.
Keep abreast of market developments, valuation assumptions and practices as well as key underwrite assumptions
Drive optimisation of the financial outcomes, assessing options and recommending strategies which underpin value and achieve a "highest and best use" analysis and best-case scenarios
Understanding of cash and capital impacts of all major decisions and investments
Research and analyse data to support underlying assumptions
Prepare competitor analyses and market intelligence reports

Your point of difference

You will be professionally qualified with demonstrated experience working as an Analyst within a development or investments environment. You will have a strong capability of building, amending and testing of financial models and experience working with large datasets and preparing reports and presentations.

You will be a strong communicator, with well-developed analytical and problem-solving skills as well as a self-motivated and inquisitive nature who thrives building and developing stakeholder relationships.

All of these attributes are preferred, if you think you may be the right fit for the role please still apply.

6 Month contract + Intended Extension paying approx. $859/day
Brisbane CBD Location with flexibility to WFH 2 days per week
Experience analysing operating processes and systems within a complex, multi-site organisation.
Our Queensland Government Client is currently seeking a Change Analyst to support the delivery of change management and communication activities across a portfolio of complex initiatives and reform programs. Reporting to the Manager of Change Management, this role contributes to the successful planning, coordination, integration, and adoption of change across the organisation.

The role is responsible for supporting overarching change governance, engaging stakeholders to build buy-in and alignment, and providing implementation and integration advice to ensure initiatives are effectively embedded into business operations. It also plays an important role in supporting workforce transition activities, business readiness, communication planning, and organisational adoption during key implementation periods.

The primary accountabilities of the Change Analyst include:

Plan and deliver change management activities in line with the organisation’s change management framework to support complex projects and reform initiatives.
Develop and maintain change impact assessments, stakeholder analysis, and other core change artefacts.
Support the development and delivery of change and communication plans aligned with program and project delivery milestones.
Coordinate stakeholder engagement activities and prepare communication materials to support awareness, understanding, and adoption.
Support business readiness activities, including training coordination, transition planning, and implementation support.
Maintain reporting inputs and divisional progress updates to support oversight of change delivery across the portfolio.
Work collaboratively with portfolio change teams, project managers, business units, and change champions to socialise, integrate, and embed change into organisational culture and operations.
Support stakeholder engagement planning, including identification of appropriate engagement approaches to achieve strategic alignment and effective adoption outcomes.
Assist with workforce transition planning, including consideration of technology impacts where relevant.
Support the transition of change initiatives into business-as-usual operations through effective collaboration across internal and external stakeholder groups.
Build and maintain strong working relationships to guide, coordinate, and integrate change activities associated with strategic priorities and reform objectives.
Provide advice to leaders and stakeholders on change progress, observations, risks, and transition readiness.
Contribute to the communication and translation of reform initiatives for key stakeholder groups in collaboration with colleagues across the broader transformation or program environment.
Provide surge support during peak delivery periods and major implementation milestones.
Contribute to a culture of collaboration, evidence-based decision-making, accountability, innovation, and high performance.
This role is suited to an experienced change professional who can operate effectively as an individual contributor within a complex program or reform environment. The successful candidate will demonstrate strong applied knowledge, skills, and experience across change delivery, communications, stakeholder engagement, and business readiness.

The ideal applicant will demonstrate:

Experience developing and delivering change management, communication, and engagement strategies within large-scale project or program environments.
Strong knowledge of contemporary and best-practice change management frameworks and methodologies.
Sound understanding of project management principles, change processes, and business integration strategies.
Experience analysing operating processes and systems within a complex, multi-site organisation.
Demonstrated capability supporting transformation initiatives across large and complex organisations.
High-level interpersonal skills, with the ability to build trusted relationships, negotiate effectively, and influence stakeholders on sensitive or complex matters.
Strong written communication skills, including experience preparing a range of clear, professional materials for diverse audiences.
The ability to work collaboratively across multidisciplinary teams to support implementation, integration, and sustained business adoption.
Highly Regarded The following would be viewed favourably:

Experience working within a complex service delivery or operational environment.
Experience supporting reform or transformation in a highly regulated, industrially sensitive, or operationally complex setting.
Industry-recognised qualifications or certification in change management.
Formal qualifications in business, human resources, project management, or a related discipline.
